commands.review_summary:
This PR adds schema, docs, runtime resolution, and tests for per-agent `agents.list[].compaction` and `contextPruning` overrides across embedded, CLI, Codex, memory, gateway, and config surfaces.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of PR head gives a high-confidence reproduction path for the blocking defects: the empty-block Codex test contradicts the resolver semantics, and the queued/CLI compaction builders omit the opt-in flag needed to apply `compaction.thinkingLevel`.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer review of the per-agent config contract after the source blockers are fixed.

target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent compaction and contextPruning overrides so each configured agent can resolve compaction/runtime behavior from its active agent scope instead of always inheriting `agents.defaults`.

This is for multi-agent installations where main, channel/proxy, local/self-hosted, and Codex/app-server agents need different compaction model/provider/auth-profile, timeout, cache/pruning, and transcript-maintenance behavior.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related: #52751, #69988, #19501, #15826, #43834, #6571, #6572, #30452, #57901, #58077, #59863, #60512, #80784, #82008

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, per-agent compaction settings were either rejected by schema or only partially honored. Several runtime paths still read `agents.defaults.compaction` / `agents.defaults.contextPruning` even when the active `agents.list[]` entry had its own settings.

That broke or weakened these setups:

- main/default agent compacts with one model/provider while another agent compacts with another
- local/self-hosted agents use local provider aliases for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit an OpenClaw compaction model override and keep native runtime behavior
- legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys still need an explicit non-main `agentId` preserved across queued/direct compaction and maintenance paths

## What Changed

- Adds strict schema/docs support for `agents.list[].compaction` and per-agent `contextPruning` semantics.
- Deep-merges per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` blocks over `agents.defaults`, so partial overrides inherit unspecified fields.
- Resolves compaction model/provider/auth-profile/timeout/cache/pruning settings through the active session agent.
- Threads `sessionAgentId` through queued/direct compaction, context-engine maintenance, Codex app-server lifecycle, embedded attempt cache-TTL finalization, memory flush, transcript rotation, and follow-up/notice paths.
- Keeps explicit `compaction.model` behavior separate from Codex/app-server native runtime selection.
- Adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent scope in direct, queued, Codex lifecycle, cache-TTL, memory-flush, and partial-merge paths.

## Reviewer Notes

The key invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR explicitly passes `agentId` into compaction and maintenance params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

The accepted per-agent contract is merge semantics: omit fields to inherit defaults; set fields to override them. `{}` means “override nothing,” not “clear inherited defaults.”

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed in current head: Per-agent compaction/contextPruning settings resolve from the active session agent in the repaired paths ClawSweeper flagged: legacy/non-agent-shaped Codex lifecycle maintenance, embedded cache-TTL finalization, and direct compaction fallback selection.
- Real environment tested for current head: Local OpenClaw source checkout on host `Nano-01`, branch rebased onto `origin/main` at `1a7669bc63a0c3731577465b130873e849fc2773`, current PR head `f60d2e761a2e0f03f1e6c04308729362bd3010f8`.
- Exact commands run after latest patch:

```text
git fetch origin main --prune
git rebase origin/main
git rev-parse HEAD
# f60d2e761a2e0f03f1e6c04308729362bd3010f8

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.agents.json --pretty false

npx oxlint \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.harness.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts

npm run config:schema:check
npm run config:docs:check
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

Evidence after latest fix:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs ...
 Test Files 2 passed (2)
 Tests 58 passed (58)
 Test Files 1 passed (1)
 Tests 15 passed (15)
 [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 71.46s

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.agents.json --pretty false
 passed

npx oxlint ...targeted files...
 Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

npm run config:schema:check
 [base-config-schema] ok

npm run config:docs:check
 OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
 passed
```

Observed result after latest fix: Current head threads `sessionAgentId` into Codex context-engine bootstrap/turn maintenance, embedded attempt cache-TTL finalization, and direct compaction fallback lookup. Regressions prove legacy Codex sessions bind maintenance to the explicit active agent, direct compaction asks fallback resolution with `agentId: "lossless-agent"` for a legacy session key, and cache-TTL helper semantics still honor per-agent contextPruning inheritance.

What was not tested in this current-head pass: This pass did not redeploy the rebased branch to the live gateway; it is source/runtime-harness proof against the exact current head. Older live-gateway proof was run earlier on `7e5f7ac` and is no longer used as the primary proof for this head.

## Supplemental Validation

Additional focused validation previously run on this branch:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
 Test Files 2 passed (2)
 Tests 56 passed (56)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts
 Test Files 4 passed (4)
 Tests 129 passed (129)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.timeout-triggered-compaction.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.context-engine.test.ts
 Test Files 7 passed (7)
 Tests 148 passed (148)
```

## Current Branch State

- Base: `origin/main` / `1a7669bc63a0c3731577465b130873e849fc2773`
- Head: `f60d2e761a2e0f03f1e6c04308729362bd3010f8`
- GitHub reports the PR as mergeable; checks are expected to be queued/running after the force-with-lease push.
